summary: The CI job failed during the build step: numerous g++/gcc compilation/linking errors occurred causing the build to abort.
root_cause: C++ compilation/linking failed during the build (multiple g++/gcc commands reported errors), causing the job to fail.

evidence.reason: Shows many errors occurred during the run, indicating a build failure rather than a transient test flake.
Repeated g++ invocation lines show compilation/linking steps failed.
gcc invocation also failed, indicating multiple C/C++ compile/link commands errored.
The build was configured with verbose and error-on-warn, showing the subsequent compile errors happened after configure.
Presence of stderr marker indicates there are error messages from the build toolchain captured in the log.
An addon build step was executed and likely contributed to the compiler errors reported.

suggested_fix: Open the full CI log around the first reported stderr/g++ failure (see the '##[error]--- stderr ---' section) to identify the exact compiler error messages; fix the underlying C/C++ compilation or linking errors (e.g., missing symbols, include paths, or incompatible flags) and re-run the build. If warnings are treated as errors due to '--error-on-warn', consider addressing or temporarily disabling those warnings while fixing the root cause.
